title: Smoky Creamy Slovakian Pork Stew
description: A rich, garlicky stew with smoked paprika and coconut cream, inspired by Slovakian comfort food, perfect for a cozy date night.

ingredients.name: pork shoulder, bone‑in, cut into 2‑inch cubes

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium‑high heat.
Add pork cubes in a single layer, searing until browned on all sides, about 5‑6 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining tablespoon of olive oil. Sauté diced onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic, sliced carrots, and celery; cook, stirring, for 2 minutes.
Return pork to the pot. Sprinkle smoked paprika over everything. Stir to coat.
If using liquid smoke, drizzle it over the mixture and stir to distribute the smoky flavor.
Pour in low‑sodium chicken broth, scraping any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer gently for 45 minutes, or until pork is tender.
Stir in unsweetened coconut cream, season with salt and pepper to taste, and heat through for another 5 minutes.
Remove from heat, top with chopped parsley, and serve hot with a side of crusty bread or a simple green salad.
